title: Nothing to Offer
alt: cerebrospinal fluid, pericardial fluid, sputum, aqueous humor, perilymph, chyme, hydatid fluid, interstitial fluid, rheum, and gin.
transcript:
[Radio on a dresser.]
Radio: I have nothing to offer
Radio: But

Radio: Blood, toil, tears, sweat,

Radio: spit, bile, vomit, urine,

[Text is now bleeding through the background and gets obscured then cut off.]
Radio: mucus, semen, earwax, lymph, gastric acid, sebum, pus, endolymph, intracellular fluid, blood plasma, vitreous humor, feces, pleural cavity fluid, chlye, synovial fluid, peritoneal fluid,
explanation:
"I have nothing to offer but " is a famous phrase in a speech given by , which was broadcast on the radio in 1940 during . The comic then goes on to list numerous other increasingly obscure , including through the title text, ending humorously with  (which Churchill was partial to).

The fluids in order: , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , and . The partially obscured ones near the end are taken by process of elimination. CHLY- seems to be a typo for CHYL(E).
